tools.name: shell_execute
tools.description: Execute shell commands securely with intelligent output handling. When output_truncated=true, use output_id with read_execution_output to get complete results. Returns partial output for immediate context while preserving full results in files. Supports adaptive execution mode that automatically switches to background for long-running commands. New: Support pipeline operations with input_output_id to use previous command output as input. NOTE: This is MCP Shell Server tool - do NOT use VS Code internal run_in_terminal parameters like "explanation" or "isBackground".

command.description: Shell command to execute (e.g., "ls -la", "npm install", "python script.py"). Command will be validated against security restrictions. NOTE: This is MCP Shell Server - do NOT use VS Code internal run_in_terminal parameters like "explanation".

comment.description: Optional comment from the LLM client explaining the intent or context behind this command execution. This helps the safety evaluator understand the broader context, but will be treated as advisory only and not blindly trusted.

execution_mode.description: How the command should be executed: "foreground" (wait for completion), "background" (run async), "detached" (fire-and-forget), "adaptive" (start foreground, switch to background for long-running commands)

working_directory.description: Directory where the command should be executed. If not specified, uses the default working directory set by shell_set_default_workdir or the initial server directory.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables to set for this command execution. These are added to or override the current environment.

input_data.description: Standard input data to provide to the command. Useful for commands that read from stdin.

input_output_id.description: Output ID from previous command execution to use as input. Alternative to input_data for pipeline operations.

timeout_seconds.description:
Global timeout (1-3600s). Default: 60s.
Per execution_mode (effective limits before execution starts):
• foreground: Schema allows 1-3600s, but the default security policy caps runs at 300s. Setting timeout_seconds above 300s raises TIMEOUT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED unless max_execution_time is increased via security_set_restrictions.
• background: 1-3600s. Intended for >300s runs; still subject to the same security cap (300s by default) unless raised.
• detached: 1-3600s. Shares the security cap behavior with background.
• adaptive: 1-3600s total cap. The initial foreground phase also respects foreground_timeout_seconds (≤300s) and the security cap.
Guidance: For long-running tasks (>300s), raise max_execution_time or use background/adaptive modes.

foreground_timeout_seconds.description:
Initial foreground window for adaptive mode (1-300s).
Behavior by execution_mode:
• adaptive: Duration to remain in foreground before automatically switching to background if the command is still running. Must be ≤ timeout_seconds.
• foreground: Does not trigger background switching (value is effectively unused for switching). Use background/adaptive for >300s scenarios.
• background/detached: Ignored.

return_partial_on_timeout.description: When timeout occurs, return partial output collected so far instead of an error. Useful for monitoring long-running commands.

max_output_size.description: Maximum output size in bytes (1KB-100MB). Output will be truncated if it exceeds this limit. Default: 5MB.

capture_stderr.description: Whether to capture standard error output in addition to stdout. When false, stderr is discarded.

session_id.description: Session ID for grouping related command executions. Used for process management and filtering in process_list.

create_terminal.description: Create a new interactive terminal session instead of running command directly. Use for commands requiring interactive input/output.

terminal_shell.description: Shell type for the new terminal (bash, zsh, fish, cmd, powershell). Only used when create_terminal is true.

terminal_dimensions.description: Terminal dimensions in characters (width x height). Only used when create_terminal is true. Default: 120x30.

force_user_confirm.description: Force user confirmation regardless of LLM evaluation result. Use this to test ELICITATION functionality or when you want direct user confirmation even for safe commands.

tools.name: process_get_execution
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific command execution, including status, output, execution time, and any errors. Use the execution_id returned from shell_execute.

execution_id.description: Unique execution ID returned by shell_execute. Use this to retrieve detailed information about a specific command execution.

tools.name: shell_set_default_workdir
tools.description: Set the default working directory for command execution

working_directory.description: Absolute path to set as the default working directory for all subsequent command executions. Must be an existing, accessible directory.

tools.name: list_execution_outputs
tools.description: List all output files generated by command executions, including stdout, stderr, and log files. Supports filtering by execution ID, output type, or filename pattern.

output_type.description: Filter by output type: "stdout" (standard output), "stderr" (error output), "combined" (both), "log" (execution logs), or "all" (no filter)

execution_id.description: Filter files by the execution that created them. Use execution_id from shell_execute results.

name_pattern.description: Filter by filename using substring match (case-insensitive). E.g., ".log" will match all log files.

limit.description: Maximum number of files to return (1-1000). Use for pagination through large file lists.

tools.name: read_execution_output
tools.description: Read complete output from command executions when output_truncated=true. Use output_id from shell_execute response to get full stdout/stderr that exceeded size limits or was cut off due to timeouts. Essential for viewing complete results of long commands or large outputs.

output_id.description: Unique output file ID from list_execution_outputs. Use this to read a specific output file generated by command execution.

offset.description: Byte offset to start reading from (0-based). Use for reading large files in chunks or continuing from previous read.

size.description: Number of bytes to read (1B-10MB). Larger sizes may improve efficiency but use more memory. Default: 8KB.

encoding.description: Character encoding for text files (utf-8, ascii, latin1, etc.). Use "binary" for non-text files.

tools.name: delete_execution_outputs
tools.description: Delete one or more output files by their output_ids. Requires explicit confirmation flag to prevent accidental deletion. Useful for cleanup after processing results.

output_ids.description: List of output file IDs to delete. Get these from list_execution_outputs. All specified files will be permanently removed.

confirm.description: Deletion confirmation flag. Must be set to true to proceed with deletion. Required to prevent accidental data loss.

tools.name: get_cleanup_suggestions
tools.description: Get automatic cleanup suggestions for output file management. Analyzes current directory size and file age to recommend cleanup candidates. Helps manage disk usage by identifying old or large files.

max_size_mb.description: Size threshold in MB for cleanup warnings. Default: 50MB.

max_age_hours.description: Age threshold in hours for cleanup candidates. Default: 24 hours.

include_warnings.description: Whether to include cleanup recommendations. Default: true.

tools.name: perform_auto_cleanup
tools.description: Perform automatic cleanup of old output files based on age and retention policies. Supports dry-run mode for safety. Automatically preserves recent files while cleaning up old ones to manage disk space.

max_age_hours.description: Files older than this (in hours) will be deleted. Default: 24 hours.

dry_run.description: If true, simulate cleanup without deleting files. Default: true for safety.

preserve_recent.description: Number of most recent files to preserve regardless of age. Default: 10.

tools.name: terminal_operate
tools.description: Unified terminal operations: create sessions, send input, get output with automatic position tracking. Combines terminal_create, terminal_send_input, and terminal_get_output into a single streamlined interface for efficient terminal workflows.

terminal_id.description: Existing terminal ID to use. If not provided, creates new terminal when command is specified.

command.description: Command to execute. Required when creating new terminal (terminal_id not provided).

dimensions.description: Terminal dimensions. For new terminals: initial size. For existing terminals: resize if different from current size.

input.description: Input to send to terminal. Can be partial input or complete command.

execute.description: Whether to press Enter after sending input (execute command)

control_codes.description: Whether to interpret input as control codes

send_to.description: Program guard target to ensure input is sent to the correct process. Can be process name, path, "pid:12345", "sessionleader:", or "*" for any process.

force_input.description: Force input even if unread output exists. Default: false (input rejected if unread output exists).

get_output.description: Whether to retrieve terminal output after operations

output_delay_ms.description: Delay in milliseconds before retrieving output (allows command to complete)

output_lines.description: Number of output lines to retrieve

response_level.description: Response detail level: minimal (only essential data, fastest), standard (balanced info for most use cases), full (complete details, maximum information)

session_name_pattern.description: Filter terminals by session name using substring match (case-insensitive). E.g., "dev" will match "development", "devtools", etc.

status_filter.description: Filter by terminal status: "active" (currently running commands), "idle" (waiting for input), "all" (no filter)

limit.description: Maximum number of terminals to return (1-200). Use for pagination through large terminal lists.

terminal_id.description: Unique terminal ID from terminal_create or terminal_list. Use this to get detailed information about a specific terminal session.
Unique terminal ID from terminal_create or terminal_list. All processes in this terminal will be terminated.

save_history.description: Whether to save the command history before closing. History can be restored when creating future terminals.

tools.name: command_history_query
tools.description: Universal command history query tool with pagination, search, individual reference, and analytics capabilities. Supports: entry references via execution_id (avoiding duplication with process_get_execution), analytics (stats/patterns/top_commands), paginated search with date filtering. Use this for all command history operations.

query.description: Search term to filter commands (case-insensitive partial match)

command_pattern.description: Filter by command using substring match (case-insensitive)

date_from.description: Filter entries from this date (ISO string)

date_to.description: Filter entries to this date (ISO string)

analytics_type.description: Type of analytics to return: "stats" (general statistics), "patterns" (user confirmation patterns), "top_commands" (most frequent commands)

include_full_details.description: Include full entry details or just metadata with IDs

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 13 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server allows external log level escalation to DEBUG
findings.description: The MCP server accepted an unauthenticated logging/setLevel request with level='debug'. This may expose tokens, internal paths, and sensitive state in log streams accessible to the operator.
findings.evidence: logging/setLevel with level='debug' and level='emergency' both returned success
findings.remediation: Restrict logging/setLevel to admin contexts or disable it entirely.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Server correctly ignores cancellation of unknown request
findings.description: The server remained responsive after receiving notifications/cancelled for a non-existent request.

findings.title: Cancellation race condition timing detected — server responded normally
findings.description: The request/cancel race did not cause a hang; the server responded to one of the two messages.
findings.remediation: No action required — server handled the race gracefully.
